content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Reviewed amendment fr-7-aa318c37 proposing tokio::time::pause() or start_paused=true for the spawn_reap_with_timeout_drops_on_expiry test. Verified the test code: it uses #[tokio::test] with wall-clock timing (50ms timeout + 300ms sleep), and the inner future is std::future::pending() with no real I/O. Using start_paused=true is the correct fix — tokio auto-advances simulated time through both the 50ms timeout and 300ms sleep deterministically, eliminating CI flakiness without changing test semantics.

## Votes

- `fr-7-aa318c37`: Accept
  Confirmed by reading the test (process_backend.rs:4160-4186) and the production function (line 2016-2029). The test's inner future uses std::future::pending() — no real I/O or system calls — so paused time is fully compatible. The production path uses tokio::time::timeout which auto-advances under paused time. The 300ms sleep in the test body also auto-advances. With start_paused=true, the entire test runs deterministically in microseconds regardless of CI load, while preserving the exact same logical assertions. This is the idiomatic tokio testing pattern for timer-dependent code and eliminates a real flakiness vector. The fix is a one-line annotation change from #[tokio::test] to #[tokio::test(start_paused = true)].
